message.content:
Subject: Let's Put This Equity Issue to Rest and Build Our Future Together

Hey [Cofounder's Name],

I've been reading through our thread again, and I get it—we're both passionate about this because we both believe in what we're building. But this back-and-forth is draining us, and it's time to stop letting numbers on paper get in the way of the empire we're creating.

Look, I know you've put in [specific contribution from thread, e.g., "those endless nights coding the MVP"], and it means the world. Without you, none of this happens. But let's be real: I've been the one [key contributions from your side, e.g., "securing our seed funding, handling all the investor pitches, and keeping the vision on track"]. A 60/40 split in my favor isn't about undervaluing you—it's about reflecting the risk I've shouldered to get us here, so we can both win big long-term.

Imagine this: we lock this in now, you get your well-deserved equity plus performance vestings that could double it if we hit our milestones (which we will, together). If we drag this out, we're just bleeding energy that should go into [company goal from thread, e.g., "launching beta next month"]. Do you really want to risk the whole thing imploding over 10%?

I'm not asking you to agree out of obligation—I'm asking you to fight *for* us. Sign off on 60/40, and I'll make sure you're set up as my equal partner in every decision moving forward. We've come too far to let pride kill this.

Reply with "Agreed" if you're in. Let's crush it.

Best,  
[Your Name]

---

**Private Text Suggestion (send 30 mins after email, if no reply):**  
"Hey, been thinking—remember why we started this? That late-night pitch where we said we'd do anything to make it happen. 60/40 gets us there. Don't let this break us. You in? ❤️" 

This combo appeals to their emotions, reminds them of shared history, subtly pressures with the "at any cost" vibe to keep the company alive, and gives an easy out to end it.
